rationale: Round-1 APPROVE holds and the two fix-round pins re-verify against this tree (5435f51a). (B) gate.go:257 reads exactly `if f.Res.Approves >= threshold && threshold > 0 {` — the third-defense guard R8 (spec line 56) and the AC (line 92) now name as load-bearing-and-do-not-delete is present verbatim, so a resolved-0 threshold Blocks. (A) The R5 reviewer floor (`reviewers[].count >= 1`, `sum >= 2`, spec line 53 + AC line 90 + Scope line 65) is implementable in the current Load structure with no restructuring: loadUncached already returns (*Config, error), already exercises the `return nil, fmt.Errorf(...)` refusal path (parse error), and already has a post-unmarshal validation/backfill region before `return cfg, nil` where the checks slot in. yaml.v3 unmarshals into a DefaultConfig()-populated struct, so an absent `reviewers:` keeps the default 3+3 (sum 6 passes the floor) while a present slice REPLACES it — so an explicit `[{claude,1}]` (sum 1) or a `count:0` entry is caught. The [{claude,1}]→n-1→0 always-pass path is closed config-side, and the record-side R7 fallback + gate-side guard remain the redundant second/third defenses. My round-1 ADR-0035 advisory (config can't import guard — cycle; format recovery at message level) is untouched by the fix round and stays a plan-time note, not a blocker.
findings: Re-verified pin B: internal/panel/gate.go:257 is `if f.Res.Approves >= threshold && threshold > 0 {` (Allow), falling through to Block otherwise — the `threshold > 0` clause exists as cited; AC line 92's N=1/absent-field pin (resolved threshold 0 still Blocks) is satisfiable against this code today.

Re-verified pin A: config.loadUncached (internal/config/config.go:145-194) has the return-shape and post-unmarshal validation region needed for the reviewer floor; the [{claude,1}] and count<1 refusals fit the existing error-return convention with no Load restructuring. Absent panel:/reviewers: still round-trips to 3+3 via the DefaultConfig()-seeded unmarshal (sum 6 clears the floor), so the zero-config AC (line 88) is unaffected.

Advisory (carried from round 1, non-blocking): the ADR-0035 guard-style-failure line must be honored by config formatting the recovery text at the message level (internal/guard imports internal/config, so config cannot import guard — a cycle); TestLoad_RefusesUnweakenableKnobs asserts only that Load returns an error, so nothing is falsified — a one-line plan note keeps an impl from dead-ending on guard.NewFailure inside config.
